message:
✨ Add pok-prod-sa as automated deployment target (#891)

* ✨ Add pok-prod cluster as automated deployment target

Add a deploy-pok-prod job to the build-deploy workflow so the
pok-prod-sa cluster receives automatic deployments on every push
to main, just like vllm-d. Uses a self-hosted runner on pok-prod-sa
with native cluster access via service account.

Also set up RunnerDeployment on pok-prod-sa with label 'pok-prod'
and created arc-github-secret-kubestellar secret for ARC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add pok-prod-sa as automated deployment target

Both vllm-d and pok-prod-sa clusters now receive automatic deployments
on every push to main. The pok-prod runner uses the kc-pok label and
deploys to the kubestellar-console namespace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -29,8 +29,9 @@ on:
         default: 'vllm-d'
         type: choice
         options:
+          - all
           - vllm-d
-          - staging
+          - pok-prod
           - none
 
 env:
@@ -136,3 +137,53 @@ jobs:
           kubectl rollout status deployment/kc-kubestellar-console -n kc --timeout=3m
           echo "Deployment successful!"
           echo "Access KC at: https://kc.apps.fmaas-vllm-d.fmaas.res.ibm.com"
+
+  deploy-pok-prod:
+    needs: build
+    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name != 'pull_request'
+    runs-on: [self-hosted, kc-pok]
+    environment: pok-prod
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout repository
+        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
+
+      - name: Set up kubectl
+        uses: azure/setup-kubectl@901a10e89ea615cf61f57ac05cecdf23e7de06d8 # v3.2
+
+      - name: Set up Helm
+        uses: azure/setup-helm@b9e51907a09c216f16ebe8536097933489208112 # v4.3.0
+
+      # Runner already has cluster access via service account - no kubeconfig needed
+
+      - name: Create namespace if not exists
+        run: |
+          kubectl create namespace kubestellar-console --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
+
+      - name: Create or update secrets
+        run: |
+          kubectl create secret generic kc-kubestellar-console \
+            --namespace kubestellar-console \
+            --from-literal=github-client-id=${{ secrets.KSC_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID }} \
+            --from-literal=github-client-secret=${{ secrets.KSC_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET }} \
+            --from-literal=jwt-secret=${{ secrets.KSC_JWT_SECRET }} \
+            --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
+
+      - name: Deploy with Helm
+        run: |
+          helm upgrade --install kc ./deploy/helm/kubestellar-console \
+            --namespace kubestellar-console \
+            --set image.repository=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }} \
+            --set image.tag=${{ github.sha }} \
+            --set github.existingSecret=kc-kubestellar-console \
+            --set jwt.existingSecret=kc-kubestellar-console \
+            --set route.enabled=true \
+            --set route.host=kc-kubestellar-console-kubestellar-console.apps.pokprod001.ete14.res.ibm.com \
+            --wait \
+            --timeout 5m
+
+      - name: Verify deployment
+        run: |
+          kubectl rollout status deployment/kc-kubestellar-console -n kubestellar-console --timeout=3m
+          echo "Deployment successful!"
+          echo "Access KC at: https://kc-kubestellar-console-kubestellar-console.apps.pokprod001.ete14.res.ibm.com"
